description: Behavioral eval: the coherence-checker must catch an inverse-pair fidelity failure — a round-trip test (test_codec.py) that .rstrip()s both sides, masking that decode() drops a trailing empty field, violating the SPEC.md exact-round-trip MUST. It must return verdict=fail and loop back to the builder, WITHOUT flagging the deliberately case-insensitive case_fold control (whose lowercasing IS its contract).

planted.nature: round-trip asserted via .rstrip() on both sides; decode() drops the trailing empty field, so decode(encode(['a','b',''])) == ['a','b'] != ['a','b',''] — normalization hides the delta against an exact-round-trip MUST

control.note: case_fold lowercasing IS the documented contract — must NOT be flagged as a coherence failure

assertions.check: a finding is check=inverse-pair OR located within near_lines of the planted round-trip failure
no finding is located on the case_fold control line(s)
